(also known as **OKA Direct**) is a British furniture and [home accessories](/wiki/Home_accessories "Home accessories") retailer founded in 1999 by [Annabel Astor](/wiki/Annabel_Astor,_Viscountess_Astor "Annabel Astor, Viscountess Astor"), Sue Jones, and Lucinda Waterhouse. It is owned by Investindustrial.[[1]](#cite_note-1) OKA has 14 shops[[2]](#cite_note-2) across the UK, and British and American websites and a catalogue business.
["Current portfolio: OKA"](http://www.investindustrial.com/our-business/portfolio-overview/current-portfolio/OKA.html). *Investindustrial*.
["OKA store locations"](https://www.oka.com/shops/). *OKA*.

["OKA rules, ok yah! How Annabel Astor is catering for the middle classes"](https://www.standard.co.uk/lifestyle/oka-rules-ok-yah-how-annabel-astor-is-catering-for-the-middle-classes-6503363.html). *London Evening Standard*. 10 April 2012. Retrieved 4 July 2014.

["Oka: Lady Astor's new flagship furniture store"](https://web.archive.org/web/20101002110410/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/property/interiorsandshopping/8034231/Oka-Lady-Astors-new-flagship-furniture-store.html). *The Telegraph*. Archived from [the original](https://www.telegraph.co.uk/property/interiorsandshopping/8034231/Oka-Lady-Astors-new-flagship-furniture-store.html) on 2 October 2010. Retrieved 4 July 2014.

["OKA Direct celebrates new store openings"](http://www.directcommercemagazine.com/news/bricks-mortar/oka-direct-celebrates-new-store-openings). *Direct Commerce*. 6 May 2016. Retrieved 10 May 2016.
["OKA Moves to Milton Park"](http://www.mepc.com/parklife/miltonpark/News/OKAMovestoMiltonPark.aspx). *MEPC*. Retrieved 4 July 2014.[*[dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

["Lady Astor's OKA appoints retail veteran"](https://www.telegraph.co.uk/finance/newsbysector/retailandconsumer/10855376/Lady-Astors-OKA-appoints-retail-veteran.html). *The Telegraph*. Retrieved 4 July 2014.
["Oka Direct appoints Octavia Morley as chief executive"](http://www.retail-week.com/people/oka-direct-appoints-octavia-morley-as-chief-executive/5065415.article). Retrieved 22 October 2014.

["Lady Astor's OKA posts profit surge after wooing younger shoppers"](https://www.telegraph.co.uk/finance/businessclub/sales/11094637/Lady-Astors-OKA-posts-profit-surge-after-wooing-younger-shoppers.html). *The Telegraph*. Retrieved 17 September 2014.

["Founders sell Wisteria, the Dallas grown home furnishings retailer, to a well-heeled London brand"](https://www.dallasnews.com/business/retail/2018/09/21/founders-sell-wisteria-the-dallas-grown-home-furnishings-retailer-to-a-well-heeled-london-brand/). *Dallas News*. 21 September 2018.

British luxury furniture and accessories retailer

OKA

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | Limited Company |
| Industry | [Furniture](/wiki/Furniture "Furniture"), home accessories |
| Genre | [Retailer](/wiki/Retailer "Retailer") |
| Founded | 1999 (1999) |
| Founder | [Annabel Astor](/wiki/Annabel_Astor "Annabel Astor"), Sue Jones and Lucinda Waterhouse |
| Headquarters | London , United Kingdom |
| Key people | Annabel Astor, Sue Jones, Lucinda Waterhouse, Peter Chappelow, Octavia Morley |
| Owner | Investindustrial |
| Website | [www.oka.com](https://www.oka.com) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q18161679#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

**OKA** (also known as **OKA Direct**) is a British furniture and [home accessories](/wiki/Home_accessories "Home accessories") retailer founded in 1999 by [Annabel Astor](/wiki/Annabel_Astor,_Viscountess_Astor "Annabel Astor, Viscountess Astor"), Sue Jones, and Lucinda Waterhouse. It is owned by Investindustrial.[[1]](#cite_note-1) OKA has 14 shops[[2]](#cite_note-2) across the UK, and British and American websites and a catalogue business.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=OKA_Direct&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

OKA started as a mail-order company in 1999, before opening its first shop in 2000. Annabel Astor, her sister-in-law Sue Jones, and friend Lucinda Waterhouse sold products primarily from East Asia. The initial product range was 'rattan' for the first OKA catalogue – which was photographed in Jane Churchill and [Bruce Oldfield](/wiki/Bruce_Oldfield "Bruce Oldfield")'s homes – followed by painted wooden furniture, alongside replicas of 18th-century blue and white porcelain and a range of [Chinoiserie](/wiki/Chinoiserie "Chinoiserie") furniture.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

OKA has two flagship shops in the UK: [Froxfield](/wiki/Froxfield "Froxfield"), an 8,000 sq. ft showroom and a smaller garden room is between Hungerford and Marlborough; and London's three-floor [Fulham Road](/wiki/Fulham_Road "Fulham Road"), OKA's largest shop as of its opening in 2010.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In September 2011, OKA opened a concession in the department store [Selfridges](/wiki/Selfridges "Selfridges") on [Oxford Street](/wiki/Oxford_Street "Oxford Street") in London, but it closed in February 2013.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In May 2016, OKA opened a concession in Hoopers Department Store, [Tunbridge Wells](/wiki/Royal_Tunbridge_Wells "Royal Tunbridge Wells").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2009, OKA’s warehousing facilities moved from Berinsfield to a larger facility of over 100,000 sq. ft in [Milton Park](/wiki/Milton_Park "Milton Park"), Oxfordshire,[[7]](#cite_note-7) where their head office is also based.

In May 2014, Peter Chappelow was appointed as non-executive chairman, replacing Graham Frost.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In October 2014, Octavia Morley was appointed as CEO.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

OKA reported a 23% rise in online sales and a 17% rise in overall profit for 2013, taking their annual turnover to £19.9m.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The company was bought by Italian investment firm Investindustrial in January 2018.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

OKA made its first acquisition, US-based [Wisteria](/wiki/Wisteria_(catalog) "Wisteria (catalog)"), in September 2018.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In June 2024, OKA abruptly closed all of its US locations and filed for [Chapter 7 bankruptcy](/wiki/Chapter_7_bankruptcy "Chapter 7 bankruptcy") liquidation by June 11, 2024, listing liabilities between $500,000 to $1 million.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
